title: Living in Boulder vlog 4 - 11 - 2017: Rode 65 miles

enrichment.summary: Kyle recaps an April 11, 2017 cycling day in Boulder: a 65-mile ride up to Jamestown and back via Apple Valley, including drafting behind a stranger on the flats. He reflects on his fitness level, recovery fatigue, quitting weed to fund better nutrition, and his renewed excitement for riding after a long break — comparing the feeling to his 2010 European bike tour.
enrichment.speaker: kyle

stances.position: Strongly prefers mountain riding over flats — finds the mountains beautiful, flats boring
Plans to quit once current supply runs out to redirect money toward cycling nutrition
Views riding someone's wheel as a practical speed calculator — saves ~30% effort, worth about 3 mph
Believes what you crave tells you what nutrients you need
Thinks bike touring is one of the best ways to get in shape because you ride all day with purpose

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday was my first day actually sizing.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I got up and felt good, like I was revived.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It took me a while to get going until around one because I had to figure out how to work my underwater camera in P3.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I experimented with the underwater camera and found out it can zoom in and out, place a song segment, do a random shuffle, and push forward, but the button volume is not very good.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I think I'll exclusively use the underwater camera for water or swimming.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I transferred music to my P3 clear and was running them.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I helped someone put big bags of dirt in her garden.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: It takes every ounce of energy I have to keep track of my stuff and put it away.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel really tired and my brain needs to work in the right way.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: When I compare myself to when I was younger, I feel the same sensations riding a bike, but I go a lot slower now.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Yesterday I was laid out for two hours until 11 because I felt exhausted.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Today I got up and went for a ride up to Jamestown.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I forgot to bring my government computer to see how fast I was going.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I thought I hit 'go' on my phone but realized later I hit 'stop' instead.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I saw an old lady pass me while riding, followed by an old guy and a triathlon girl.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I got cold riding in shorts and a t-shirt, so I put on a windbreaker.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I prefer riding in the mountains more than on the flats.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to do a mountain ride tomorrow and bring a jacket.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I enjoyed riding down because it's easier and fun doing corners.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I saw a dude on a bike and we hit a stop sign at the same time, then he passed me.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I wish I knew how fast we were going to calculate my level.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: If you're riding at 100% effort behind someone, your level is three miles an hour below their speed.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I couldn't ride out of the saddle because my left thigh would cramp.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I took a dirt road home and forgot how much it would slow me down.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Cobbles and rough roads slow you down more if you're heavy.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I was so tired after the ride that I had to lie down for three hours.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I went to the store and spent $30 on food, craving fruit.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I believe the best nutrients are whatever you crave.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to quit smoking weed as soon as I run out of what I have.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I want to use the money I would spend on weed on extra food.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I ate 10 goops and two power bars during my ride.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I called my mom and asked her to give me more money so I don't have to think about finances.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I feel like I'm visiting Boulder for the first time even though I live here because I haven't ridden much.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I rebuilt the mining communities on Google Earth and will show it soon.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Before the miners came, there were flowers of gold sticking out of the rocks and rivers in Boulder.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to do a video tomorrow showing where I am and riding by.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I spaced doing a video today but will do it tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I plan to try to ride to Eldor tomorrow.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I need to fix my gears because I can only get to the second highest cog.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I know how to get in shape quickly because I grew up riding and did bike touring.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: Bike touring in Europe in 2010 was great for getting in shape because you ride all day long.

claims.subject: Kyle
claims.claim: I'm looking forward to tomorrow's ride.
